name: Phenol sensor (DmpR + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects phenol and methylphenols in water/soil. The DmpR aromatic sensor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in the phenol-tolerant chassis P. putida KT2440.

input.detects: Phenolic pollutants in industrial effluent and contaminated water

sensing.sensorMechanism: DmpR is a sigma-54-dependent activator that binds phenol effectors and activates the Po promoter.
sensing.signalFlow: Phenol -> DmpR activates Po -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises with phenol.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

performance.notes: DmpR aromatic sensing and CRISPRa are validated separately; integrated device is a design template. P. putida natively tolerates aromatics.

safety.biocontainment: P. putida KT2440 certified HV1 safety host; add containment for field use.
safety.notes: Phenol-tolerant chassis works in real contaminated samples.

buildSteps.title: Design CRISPRa sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Target a weak reporter promoter; check host off-targets.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: DmpR + Po -> sgRNA. TU2: dCas9-omega. TU3: weak promoter -> sfGFP. SEVA vectors.

buildSteps.title: Expose to sample
buildSteps.description: Add water/soil eluate + phenol standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the DmpR phenol-sensing module with P. putida CRISPRa
partsSources: Shingler et al. / DmpR phenol sensing (Pseudomonas dmp)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
